summary: Excellent soundboard recording captures a stellar 1977 show highlighted by a smoking Wharf Rat and Jerry’s fiery guitar work.
review: Fans rave that this November‑6‑1977 performance is one of the band’s finest, with Garcia “on fire” all night and a particularly glowing Wharf Rat that builds into an explosive climax. The soundboard source is praised as clear and well‑balanced, making every tight jam—from Jack Straw to Samson & Delilah—easy to hear.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers give 4–5 stars and enthusiastically praise both the musical performance and the soundboard quality; no negative comments were noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers repeatedly mention great, clear sound with no reported drop‑outs or muddiness; the mix is well balanced and captures the band’s dynamics.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is described as creative and laid‑back, while the second set builds intensity, climaxing with a smoking Wharf Rat and ending on an energetic Johnny B. Goode encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is repeatedly called “on fire,” “top of the mix,” and gives listeners chills.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum segment in the St. Stephen > Drums transition is highlighted as part of a powerful medley.
